title: Interpret Division of Fractions
why: Before you can interpret what fraction division means, you need to picture equal groups and equal sharing — the same ideas behind whole-number division. Get those warm and "how many halves fit in 3?" makes sense.
skills: How many of this fit in that

diagnostic.q: In the fraction 3/4, which number is the denominator (total equal parts)?
12 ÷ 3 means: how many groups of 3 are in 12. What is it?

learn.heading: What does division mean?
learn.intro: Division can ask two things: how many equal groups (12 ÷ 3 = how many 3s in 12) or how big is each share (12 ÷ 3 = share 12 into 3 equal piles). Both ideas carry straight into fractions.
examples: How many fit: 6 ÷ 2 asks "how many 2s are in 6?" → 3.
Fraction version: "How many halves are in 1 whole?" → 2, because two 1/2 pieces make a whole.

tiers.intro: Let's count equal groups one step at a time.

items.q: A. How many equal groups of 2 are in 6? Fill in: 2 × ___ = 6
B. How many halves make 1 whole pizza?
items.hint: Two 1/2 pieces snap together to make 1 whole.

tiers.intro: Read what the division is asking, then answer.

items.q: A. How many fourths (1/4) make 1 whole?
B. 15 ÷ 3 means "how many 3s are in 15?" What is it?

tiers.intro: Stretch right up to fraction division.

items.q: A. How many halves (1/2) are in 2 whole pizzas?
items.hint: Each whole holds 2 halves, and 2 wholes → 2 × 2 = 4 halves.

items.q: B. How many thirds (1/3) are in 1 whole?

opts.t: How many groups of 4 are in 8?

exit.hint: Division asks how many equal groups fit — and the answer is 2.

exit.q: 2. How many halves (1/2) are in 1 whole?
